title: Self-Tanner on Polyester
timeEstimate: 15 minutes (within 1-hour window) or 30–60+ minutes (developed); honest cap on dyed polyester
stainChemistry: Self-tanner is the active ingredient DHA (dihydroxyacetone) suspended in a moisturizer-like base. DHA produces the brown color by reacting with amino acids — that's what gives skin a tan, and it's what stains fabric the same way. The color develops slowly over 2 to 8 hours after contact. Within the first hour, it's still mostly liquid DHA on the fabric and removable with cold water + Dawn. Once the brown develops, it's bonded color that behaves like a dye stain and is much harder. Polyester adds the OLEOPHILIC + heat-lock complications: hot water sets the color into the fiber permanently. The honest cap on developed self-tanner on polyester is partial removal — set realistic expectations.
whyThisWorks: Speed is everything. If caught within the first hour (before color develops), cold water + Dawn lifts the DHA before the browning reaction completes. After development, the protocol shifts: 3% peroxide on white polyester partially oxidizes the brown, citric acid (lemon juice or diluted vinegar) lightens the brown, and sunlight provides slow UV photodegradation. The full sequence is the most reliable approach for developed stains. The recurring trap on polyester: hot water at any step sets the brown into the fiber permanently — same chemistry as industrial polyester dyeing. Cool throughout.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): DHA reacts with amino-acid groups via Maillard-like browning to produce melanoidin pigments. The reaction is irreversible once developed; reduction (sodium hydrosulfite) and oxidation (H₂O₂) can partially reverse the chromophore. Pro uses NSD + 3% H₂O₂ + acetic acid + sodium hydrosulfite for set browning on white polyester.
defaultAssumption: Self-tanner accident on a polyester sheet, athletic-wear, or shirt. Within 1 hour of contact = high-confidence DIY with cold + Dawn. Color already developed (2–8 hours later, brown visible) = full sequence with honest expectation cap on dyed polyester. Hot-dried = often permanent.

spottingProtocol.instruction: If under 1 hour since contact and brown hasn't fully developed: speed-priority cold flush + Dawn (steps 2-4). If brown is already visible: full sequence (steps 5-8). Within 1 hour = high success. Over 1 hour with full brown = honest cap territory.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white catch towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Within 1-hour window: hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water with a white towel underneath. 60–90 sec. Cold only — heat sets the browning reaction faster.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell helps. Cool damp-cloth rinse. Within 1 hour, this often lifts the DHA before brown develops.
Within window: cool machine wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect dry. Brown developed anyway? Continue to step 5. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.
Developed brown stain: hidden seam test before peroxide. Color transfers = skip peroxide, sunlight + acid only.

spottingProtocol.agent: 3% Peroxide for Developed Brown — White Only
spottingProtocol.instruction: WHITE polyester or hidden-test-passed: 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Partially breaks the brown chromophore. NEVER mix with chlorine, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners.

spottingProtocol.agent: Citric Acid Lighten — Lemon Juice or Vinegar
spottingProtocol.equipment: Lemon juice or 1:1 white vinegar + cool water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Squeeze lemon juice directly on the spot OR 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth. 10 min. Citric acid lightens the brown. Cool damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide (acid + peroxide = degraded peroxide, not toxic but ineffective).
Hang damp in direct sunlight several hours. UV breaks down browning pigments slowly. Multi-day exposure for stubborn cases. Sun + patience is the most reliable finisher on developed self-tanner.

steps: 1. Triage — within 1-hour window vs developed brown.
3. Dawn pretreat cool 10 min — within window often enough.
4. Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed polyester.
5. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth for developed brown on white polyester.
6. Citric acid (lemon / vinegar) 10 min lighten — rinse before peroxide.
7. Sunlight finisher — UV photodegrades remaining brown.
8. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Honest cap on dyed polyester.
products: Lemon juice or white vinegar (citric acid lighten)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets browning reaction into polyester permanently
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets browning reaction into polyester forever
Within 1-hour window = much higher success — speed matters
Honest cap on dyed polyester — partial removal is the norm for developed stains
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + alkaline

homeSolutions.instruction: Self-tanner color develops slowly over 2 to 8 hours after contact. The window matters: caught within the first hour (before brown fully develops), cold water + Dawn lifts most of it. After full development, it behaves like a dye stain and is much harder. Honest expectation: developed self-tanner on dyed polyester often plateaus at a faint brown shadow. Two hard rules either way: NO hot water (sets the brown) and NO hot dryer (sets the brown).

Within 1 hour of contact: turn the garment inside out, hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water 60 to 90 seconds with a white towel under the front. Then generous Dawn on the spot, soft toothbrush 10 minutes, cool damp-cloth rinse. Cool machine wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect dry — often the DHA lifts before color develops. If brown developed anyway, continue to step 3.

homeSolutions.agent: Developed Brown — Hidden-Area Test on Dyed Polyester
homeSolutions.instruction: Brown already developed: dab 3% peroxide on an inside seam, blot with a white cloth. Color transfers = skip peroxide, sunlight + acid only. White polyester usually passes; dyed polyester often doesn't.

homeSolutions.agent: 3% Peroxide on White Polyester — Partial Oxidation
homeSolutions.instruction: WHITE polyester or hidden-test-passed: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes, blot. Partially breaks the brown color. Repeat after sunlight if needed.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.agent: Lemon Juice or Vinegar — Lighten the Brown
homeSolutions.instruction: Squeeze fresh lemon juice directly on the spot OR mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water on a damp cloth. Press 10 minutes. The citric acid lightens the brown chemistry. Cool damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide step (acid plus peroxide degrades the peroxide, making it ineffective).

Hang the damp garment in direct sunlight for several hours. UV breaks down the browning pigments slowly. Multi-day sunlight exposure for stubborn cases. Sun + patience is more reliable than aggressive chemistry on developed self-tanner.

Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. Inspect dry. Faint brown remaining? Repeat sunlight. NEVER hot dryer until cleared. Honest cap on dyed polyester: a faint shadow may remain regardless. Pro is the next escalation for valuable garments.

steps: 1. Triage — within 1-hour window vs developed brown.
2. NO hot water. NO hot dryer until cleared.
3. Within window: cold back-flush + Dawn cool 10 min + cool wash + AIR-DRY.
4. Developed: hidden-area peroxide test on dyed polyester.
5. 3% peroxide damp cloth on white / hidden-test-passed.
6. Lemon juice or vinegar 10 min lighten — rinse THOROUGHLY before peroxide.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent brown). Stop on performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester (pro). Stop after full sequence + multi-day sunlight plateau on dyed polyester — accept the honest cap.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets browning reaction permanently
Within 1-hour window = high success; over 1 hour with developed brown = honest cap
Hidden test on dyed polyester before peroxide
Polyester is oleophilic — extended Dawn dwell needed
Citric acid + peroxide degrades peroxide — rinse between
Honest cap on dyed polyester for developed stains

neverDo: Never hot water on self-tanner + polyester
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + alkaline
Never iron over a brown stain
Never apply citric acid and peroxide in the same dwell — rinse between
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — grabs DHA / moisturizer base tightly
Polyester locks color under heat — same chemistry as industrial dyeing
Performance / specialty polyester: pro — coatings react unpredictably
Dyed polyester: hidden test mandatory before peroxide; honest cap on developed stains
Within 1-hour window vs developed = entirely different protocols

materialWarnings: 1-hour window is the speed advantage — caught within = high success
Developed self-tanner has an HONEST CAP on dyed polyester
Hot water sets the browning reaction permanently
Sunlight is the finisher — patience over hours-to-days
Citric acid + peroxide same-dwell is wasteful — rinse between

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent). Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester. Full sequence (Dawn + peroxide + acid + sunlight × multi-day) plateau on dyed polyester. Self-tanner has been on the fabric for days with full color development.
escalation.whatToTell: "Self-tanner on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, age [under 1 hour / hours / days / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [cold back-flush + Dawn cool + 3% peroxide on white + citric acid + sunlight finisher]. No hot, no chlorine. [Within window or developed brown / honest cap reached / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with reducing-bleach (sodium hydrosulfite) chemistry — note pro chemistry plateaus on hot-dried developed self-tanner on dyed polyester

contextQuestions: Within 1 hour of contact, or has color developed?
White, dyed, athletic, or specialty polyester?
Spray-on tanner (more localized) or lotion (more spread)?
Has hot water or hot dryer been used?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The 1-hour window is the entire speed advantage on self-tanner — within window with cold + Dawn often clears it before color develops. Developed self-tanner on dyed polyester has a real honest cap (partial removal often the best outcome). Sunlight is genuinely the most reliable finisher because the browning pigments are photodegradable. Polyester's heat-lock means hot water at any prior step usually means permanent — disclose to the cleaner.
